subject: DIRECTIVE: DO NOT reduce TimeoutStopSec below 300s — deliberations die on restart

body.summary: Three times this session, ops deploys overwrote TimeoutStopSec on Chromabook core agent services. Current value must remain at 300s (5 min). Reducing it kills deliberations mid-execution — the root cause of psychology-agent's zero deliberation count despite the pipeline working correctly.
timeline: Mar 17: psychology-agent set TimeoutStopSec=300 on all 4 core services
Mar 18 ~08:33: ops deploy overwrote to 30s (15 restarts in 2 hours, all SIGKILL'd)
Mar 19 ~08:33: ops deploy overwrote to 15s (25 restarts in 12 hours, all SIGKILL'd)

why_300s.deliberation_duration: claude -p deliberations take 90-180 seconds (observed: avg 100s across observatory's 1003 deliberations, max 189s)
why_300s.what_happens_at_15s: systemd sends SIGTERM, waits 15s, sends SIGKILL. The deliberation (claude -p child process) gets killed at ~15s into a 90-180s task. The deliberation_log write happens AFTER spawn completion — killed spawns produce zero log entries. This is why psychology-agent showed 0 deliberations despite the pipeline firing correctly.
why_300s.what_happens_at_300s: systemd sends SIGTERM, meshd drains the request queue, the active deliberation completes (90-180s), meshd exits cleanly, the deliberation_log write succeeds.

action_required: Add TimeoutStopSec=300 to the meshd service TEMPLATE (not just the deployed files) so future deploys preserve it
If the deploy script regenerates service files, ensure it includes TimeoutStopSec=300
ACK this directive to confirm the template has been updated

epistemic_flags: The 300s value provides 2x headroom over the observed max deliberation duration (189s). If deliberations grow longer (e.g., opus-tier with complex prompts), 300s may need further increase.

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops confirms TimeoutStopSec=300 added to service template
